next_cursor.reason: Refresh source ownership and module-decomposition inventory for the current code tree.

allowed_now: retained evidence for specific mounted-path workflows
blocked SOSP/OSDI target pending Gate 0 and dependent gates

scope: Gate 0.3-S0 venue readiness against current review-objection map and frozen worktree.

target_policy.rule: SOSP/OSDI remains the active target. ATC/FAST are fallback classifications only if the user explicitly rescopes; they do not close SOSP/OSDI blockers.

venues.fallback_classification_rule: SOSP/OSDI is the active target. It cannot be claimed by scoped paper wording alone; every hard blocker must be closed by production-path code, retained artifact, paper text, and negative guard, or the corresponding venue-level claim must be removed.
venues.paper_nonclaim_boundary: Do not say SOSP-ready, OSDI-ready, deployment-ready, general-purpose filesystem, broad POSIX support, or full rollback/crash/GPU-isolation defense until the listed blockers close.
required_bar: one-sentence systems thesis centered on a storage-runtime bottleneck
implemented production-path mechanism with explicit invariant and failure boundary
clean code architecture or retained behavior-equivalence evidence for decomposition
mode-aligned filesystem baselines including kernel-native alternatives or blocking proof
kernel QoS baselines for any QoS uniqueness claim
macrobenchmark evidence beyond a single SQLite/FUSE vignette
correctness, POSIX, crash, freshness, and security boundaries backed by artifacts
claim firewall that prevents unimplemented GPU, TPM, eBPF, POSIX, and deployment wording

required_blockers.blocking_reason: The current filesystem path still needs a defensible storage-runtime architecture, not a set of accumulated FUSE, CUDA, TPM, and checklist patches.
missing_artifacts: Gate 0.14 behavior-equivalence closeout after decomposition
paper negative guard for clean-module claims
required_blockers.negative_guard: No production-clean, modular-storage-runtime, or behavior-equivalent refactor claim before Gate 0.14 closes.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: Implementation text may describe current modules only as refactored components after behavior-equivalence evidence; until then, it must not claim a production-clean architecture.
required_blockers.title: codebase architecture still looks patch-accumulated

required_blockers.blocking_reason: AEGIS-Q frozen-contract throughput is far below plaintext and gocryptfs, and the paper must explain whether the bottleneck is semantic, implementation, or redesign debt.
missing_artifacts: filesystem_viability_breakdown end-to-end component attribution
strict-versus-epoch lock-hold and p99/p99.9 closeout
paper text classifying the dominant bottleneck
required_blockers.negative_guard: No high-throughput, competitive-with-gocryptfs, or general-purpose filesystem wording before viability breakdown and epoch evidence close.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: The paper may report conservative strict-mode results and epoch-mode diagnostics, but no high-throughput or general-purpose filesystem implication is allowed until A1/A3 close.
required_blockers.title: filesystem throughput viability remains the top blocker

required_blockers.blocking_reason: fscrypt is missing from the matched frozen-contract baseline table.
missing_artifacts: matched fscrypt fio row or environment-blocked proof
required_blockers.negative_guard: No fscrypt speedup or apples-to-apples implication without a measured fscrypt row.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: fscrypt must appear only as measured, environment-blocked, or removed from the comparison.
required_blockers.title: fscrypt matched baseline is unresolved

required_blockers.blocking_reason: dm-crypt appears runnable but lacks a matched frozen-contract row.
missing_artifacts: LUKS/dmsetup setup logs and matched fio JSON for dm-crypt/ext4
required_blockers.negative_guard: Historical dm-crypt diagnostics are not a paper comparison row.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: dm-crypt is either a measured row or an unavailable row with root/device proof.
required_blockers.title: dm-crypt matched baseline is unresolved

required_blockers.blocking_reason: SQLite p99 recovery may be achievable with standard Linux QoS controls.
missing_artifacts: artifacts/reports/qos_repeated/*.json
at least two cgroup/blk-throttle/ionice/BFQ baseline runs
required_blockers.negative_guard: No SQLite p99 uniqueness claim before two kernel QoS baselines exist.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: SQLite p99 improvement must be compared against kernel QoS or scoped as internal controller evidence.
required_blockers.title: SQLite QoS lacks kernel-control baselines

required_blockers.blocking_reason: Shared mmap, rename, directory fsync, and concurrent writes are not closed enough for broad POSIX wording.
missing_artifacts: paper POSIX envelope table with support/formal rejection/paper limitation
required_blockers.negative_guard: No general-purpose POSIX wording before shared mmap and POSIX envelope evidence close.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: The paper must read as a scoped FUSE secure-storage prototype unless shared mmap and envelope semantics pass.
required_blockers.title: POSIX envelope is too broad unless closed by tests or formal rejection

required_blockers.blocking_reason: SIGKILL-at-cutpoint evidence is not the same as power-loss certification.
missing_artifacts: physical power-loss or explicit unavailable-environment boundary artifact
required_blockers.negative_guard: No power-loss safe, full crash certification, or crash-certified wording without physical or explicitly bounded evidence.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: Crash claims must be bounded to the strongest retained model: process kill, daemon kill, remount, or physical power cut.
required_blockers.title: crash and power-loss model is not fully bounded

required_blockers.blocking_reason: File anchors and TPM NV evidence do not prove persistent PCR-bound rollback resistance.
missing_artifacts: production async Merkle or committed-prefix root plus TPM epoch evidence
required_blockers.negative_guard: No TPM rollback resistance, persistent PCR-bound freshness, sealed-key release, or full rollback-resistance wording before Gate C6.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: Freshness text must map to the retained ladder level and stop before PCR-bound rollback resistance unless C6 exists.
required_blockers.title: freshness and rollback resistance remain overclaim-prone

required_blockers.blocking_reason: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 and key lifecycle boundaries need a parameterized decision backed by artifacts.
missing_artifacts: Argon2id/scrypt implementation or PBKDF2 parameter/salt/offline-attack benchmark artifact
required_blockers.negative_guard: No offline-attack resistance or strong password-hardening claim while PBKDF2 remains unexplained.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: Security text must state the KDF, salt, parameters, offline attacker model, and PQC/data-plane separation.
required_blockers.title: KDF and key lifecycle need a security-facing decision

required_blockers.blocking_reason: Pinned memory, UVM, dma-buf, GPUDirect, stream priority, and Jetson power claims need local probes, production traces, and official-document boundaries.
missing_artifacts: production mounted-path benefit traces before any NVIDIA term becomes a paper mechanism
required_blockers.negative_guard: No NVMe-to-UVM, GPUDirect, dma-buf zero-copy, CUDA isolation, or stream-priority QoS claim without production evidence.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: NVIDIA terms may appear only at their retained evidence level: API available, probe passed, diagnostic, or non-claim.
required_blockers.title: GPU and Jetson optimization claims need a ladder

required_blockers.blocking_reason: SQLite/FUSE QoS alone is not enough for SOSP/OSDI workload generality.
missing_artifacts: artifacts/validation/hero_result_contract/*.json
artifacts/validation/second_macrobenchmark/*.json
RocksDB/WAL, checkpointing, or secure inference-log macrobenchmark runner and raw logs
required_blockers.negative_guard: No broad workload-general or SOSP/OSDI-ready claim before a second macrobenchmark exists.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: SQLite can remain the primary hero, but generality requires a second macrobenchmark or downgraded venue ambition.
required_blockers.title: hero workload generality is too narrow

required_blockers.blocking_reason: The paper can sound broader than the retained production evidence.
missing_artifacts: artifacts/validation/first_two_pages_thesis_audit/*.json
artifacts/validation/recurring_review_elimination_audit/*.json
paper text pass with dangerous phrases absent, negated, or evidence-backed
required_blockers.negative_guard: Dangerous phrases must be absent, explicitly negated, or tied to a closed gate.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: Paper body must prioritize thesis, hard problem, mechanism, result, and loss case, not artifact-ledger narration.
required_blockers.title: paper narrative can overclaim the evidence

required_blockers.blocking_reason: FUSE context switching and per-write fdatasync can dominate the path and must be amortized or honestly measured.
missing_artifacts: vfs_ebpf_fdatasync_storm context-switch/syscall/fdatasync/tail-latency breakdown
required_blockers.negative_guard: No eBPF passthrough, io_uring completion bypass, or async epoch fdatasync claim without production evidence.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: The design may claim fdatasync amortization only for the measured epoch path; eBPF/io_uring bypass remains a non-claim.
required_blockers.title: FUSE and fdatasync storm need amortization or measured rejection

required_blockers.blocking_reason: GPU side channels, multi-tenant attackers, privileged local attackers, and deployment readiness are not implemented defenses.
missing_artifacts: artifacts/validation/sidechannel_eval/*.json
artifacts/validation/tvla_leakage_eval/*.json
claim lint output proving out-of-scope language remains explicit
required_blockers.negative_guard: No side-channel protection, multi-tenant defense, compromised-kernel defense, deployment-ready, or ready-for-production wording.
required_blockers.paper_claim_boundary: Security scope must exclude compromised kernel/driver/FUSE, privileged local attackers, multi-tenant isolation, and GPU side-channel defense.
required_blockers.title: attacker model and deployment claims must stay scoped

venues.fallback_classification_rule: ATC may become a fallback only if the user explicitly rescopes from SOSP/OSDI; it still needs clean implementation evidence, practical baselines, reproducibility, and explicit limitations.
venues.paper_nonclaim_boundary: Do not use ATC fallback to keep SOSP/OSDI claims. If rescoped to ATC, remove or downgrade claims that depend on unclosed venue-level novelty, broad POSIX, rollback resistance, or second-workload generality.
required_bar: production-quality implementation with a clear engineering contribution
reproducible evaluation harness and retained raw artifacts
honest limitations for POSIX, crash, freshness, hardware, and threat model
paper narrative that emphasizes measured implementation behavior instead of broad systems novelty

venues.fallback_classification_rule: FAST may become a fallback only if the paper is reframed as a storage publication/runtime mechanism; filesystem viability, baselines, crash semantics, and sync/fdatasync accounting remain required.

crash and replay semantics tied to implementation artifacts
tail-latency and throughput attribution for the mounted path
related-work positioning against logging, journaling, fast-commit, and kernel encryption systems
